title: Imaging apparatus and control method thereof
abstract: An imaging unit of an imaging apparatus comprises an imaging element having a plurality of photoelectric conversion units that converts an optical image of an object formed by an imaging optical system into an image signal. A focus detection unit reads out a plurality of image signals that has been pupil-split by the imaging element and performs focus detection. If an accumulation time of the imaging element or a signal-to-noise ratio of the image signal changes, a system control unit controls a readout line that obtains the image signals in a focus detection region with respect to the imaging unit to change the readout line of focus detection pixels in accordance with an accumulation time or a sensitivity setting of the imaging element. In a low shutter speed and a high sensitivity, the readout control of the image signal is performed by reducing the number of readout lines.
claims: Claims ( 18 ) An imaging apparatus comprising: an imaging element configured to have a plurality of photoelectric conversion units that converts an optical image of an object formed by an imaging optical system into an image signal; a focus detection unit configured to read out a plurality of image signals that has been pupil-split by the imaging element and perform focus detection; and a control unit configured to perform control of changing a readout line that obtains the image signals in a region where the focus detection unit performs focus detection and perform control of focus adjustment of the imaging optical system by using the image signal obtained from the readout line if an accumulation time of the imaging element or a signal-to-noise ratio of the image signal changes. The imaging apparatus according to claim 1, wherein the control unit performs control of changing the number of readout lines if an exposure time or a sensitivity setting of the imaging element has changed. The imaging apparatus according to claim 2, wherein the control unit performs control of changing the number of lines to a first line number if the exposure time of the imaging element is short and the control unit performs control of changing the number of lines to a second line number that is larger than the first line number if the exposure time of the imaging element is long. The imaging apparatus according to claim 2, wherein the control unit performs control of changing the number of lines to a first line number if the sensitivity of the imaging element is low and the control unit performs control of changing the number of lines to a second lines number that is larger than the first line number if the sensitivity of the imaging element is high. The imaging apparatus according to claim 1, wherein the image signals are a first image signal and a second image signal that has been added in the imaging element, or a first image signal and a second image signal that is read out independently from the first image signal, and wherein the focus detection unit obtains the first image signal in the readout line before obtaining the second image signal. The imaging apparatus according to claim 1, wherein the image signals are added in adjacent lines in the vertical direction of a screen. The imaging apparatus according to claim 2, wherein the control unit performs control of shortening a standby period of time of the imaging element if the number of readout lines is increased. The imaging apparatus according to claim 2, wherein the control unit performs control of extending a frame period of time of a moving image if the number of the readout lines is increased. The imaging apparatus according to claim 2, wherein, if the number of the readout lines is increased, the control unit performs control of extending a frame period of time so as to encompass a readout time corresponding to the increase in the number of the readout lines in a mode in which the captured images are sequentially displayed. The imaging apparatus according to claim 2, wherein if the number of readout lines is changed, the control unit restricts an amount of change of the number of lines between consecutive frames. The imaging apparatus according to claim 1, wherein the control unit performs a process of detecting an object region of a captured image and performs control of changing the readout line if the position of the object region is changed. An imaging apparatus comprising: an imaging element configured to have a plurality of photoelectric conversion units that converts an optical image of an object formed by an imaging optical system into an image signal; a focus detection unit configured to read out a plurality of image signals that has been pupil-split by the imaging element and perform focus detection; and a control unit configured to perform control of extending a frame period of time of a moving image that obtains the image signals in a region where the focus detection unit performs focus detection and perform control of focus adjustment of the imaging optical system by using the image signal obtained from the readout line if an accumulation time of the imaging element or a signal-to-noise ratio of the image signal changes. The imaging apparatus according to claim 12, wherein the control unit performs control of extending the frame period of time of the moving image if the exposure time or the sensitivity setting of the imaging element has changed. The imaging apparatus according to claim 13, wherein the control unit sets the frame period of time of the moving image to a first moving image frame period of time if the exposure time of the imaging element is short, and the control unit performs control of extending the frame period of time of the moving image to a second moving image frame period of time that is longer than the first moving image frame period of time if the exposure time of the imaging element is long. The imaging apparatus according to claim 13, wherein the control unit sets the frame period of time of the moving image to a first moving image frame period of time if the sensitivity of the imaging element is low and the control unit performs control of extending the frame period of time of the moving image to a second moving image frame period of time that is longer than the first moving image frame period of time if the sensitivity of the imaging element is high. The imaging apparatus according to claim 12, wherein the image signals are added in adjacent lines in the vertical direction of the screen. The imaging apparatus according to claim 13, wherein, if the frame period of time of the moving image is extended, the control unit restricts an amount of change of the frame period of time of the moving image between consecutive frames. A control method executed by an imaging apparatus including an imaging element having a plurality of photoelectric conversion units that converts an optical image of an object formed by an imaging optical system into an image signal comprising: performing focus detection by reading out a plurality of image signals that has been pupil-split by the imaging element; and performing a control that obtains the image signals from a readout line in a region where focus detection is performed in performing the focus detection, and performs control of focus adjustment of the imaging optical system, wherein, in performing the control, if an accumulation time of the imaging element or a signal-to-noise ratio of the image signal changes, a control of changing the readout line is performed.
